essence: Chiang Mai builder crew aligns on VibeDeck as an Omi-style open-source macro pad for AI workflows: everyone installs and hardens the current codebase, drops per-key LCDs for one screen + six buttons + dials, sells $159 3D-printed dev kits in 100-unit batches with GitHub/Discord community seeding, and targets a Feb 7 Claude event demo before a later premium Jetson-class device.

actions.text: Set up GitHub org and push the mono-repo (app, plugins, website), then make it public before Feb 7
Share the exact Ajazz deck link so the whole team buys the same device and starts daily testing/hardening
Finalize marketing website with Stripe preorders and spin up the Discord community
Prepare open-source dev-kit lightning-talk demo for the February 7 Claude event

essence: David pairs with Angela on WSL/VSCode/Claude setup and JSON-to-HTML visualization of her north-star second-brain doc, hits the background-agents-cannot-write-without-dangerous-permissions limitation (declines to enable it), fixes her git push/rebase access, and — after seeing her weekend AMS incident dashboard — reframes incident enhancement around atomic predicate/classification signals confirmed by a human before any enhanced narrative is written.

actions.text: Review Angela's AMS reporting dataset together tomorrow and distill it into predicate/classification prompt structures for Penny the prompt engineer
Redesign the enhanced-review screen from a single enhanced narrative to human-confirmable true/false and classification signals

essence: Birthday-weekend catch-up call: David recounts the mountain trip with Joy (after a rental scam and battery failure), his channel hitting 3,100 subs and 132 Skool members, while UK friend Dave shares his father's passing, a CUPRA/VW lemon-car refund fight heading to QCAT, and gets pointed at Descript/Gling-style tools to lower his YouTube editing barrier.

David directs Claude to resolve a stale handover/plan via git history, classify the item under the BMAD v4 workflow (epic-0 story vs bug), and run a full comparison of his .claude + bmad-core config — including the Taylor story-acceptance-testing agent and the appydave multi-agent story workflow — before changing anything.

actions.text: Compare David's .claude file and bmad-core (Taylor agent, appydave workflow, KDD changes) against the project's setup and report differences before any changes

essence: Bug report: after regenerating an incident's fill form, deleting events, and regenerating the narrative, the regenerate-questions button produced new Q&A questions but kept the answers from the old questions — regeneration should invalidate prior answers.

actions.text: Fix Q&A regeneration so regenerated questions clear/invalidate previously stored answers

essence: David floats renaming the standalone 'voice agent' app (candidate names like 'The Quick Fox') and relocating it into the FliVideo/FliHub repository ecosystem, noting the codebase name refactor and folder restructure that entails.

actions.text: Rename the voice-agent codebase and move it into the FliVideo repository area

essence: David directs a unified problem/task view with at most one or two groups spanning KDD and retrospective items, a rebuild of the incomplete Epic 3 index (per index-page formatting docs), a CLAUDE.md entry point that reliably routes to project status, and distrust-driven verification of commit flows after the fill-Q&A developer button errored right after being told everything worked.

actions.text: Rebuild the Epic 3 index file with missing sections per index-page formatting documentation
Verify commit flows and debug the fill-Q&A developer-experience button error

essence: David rejects the current data grouping and naming as 'a complete mess' (the data itself is fine) and warns against delegating the restructure to the architect agent because that would route it through ChatGPT, which he does not want.

actions.text: Rework the data grouping structure and names without delegating to the architect/ChatGPT

essence: David orders a software-architect-perspective audit of all project documents — planning docs, learnings, PRDs, epics, stories, best practices — moving each file to its correct location, designing the overall information architecture, and optionally using background agents (and stop capitalizing document names).

actions.text: Audit all documents and reorganize them into a coherent information architecture, optionally via background agents

essence: With 11% context left, David logs the VA modal-focus bug on his three-monitor setup, decides E4 KDD retrospective stays separate from Epic 4 while E3 KDD items should fold into Epic 3 as submenus, picks the systematic fix over quick-fix or nuclear refactor, and mandates handovers written inline in the conversation with absolute file paths — never as new files.

actions.text: Write an inline handover specification with absolute file paths and continue the E3 KDD restructure in a fresh conversation
Fold E3 KDD index/tab/documents into Epic 3 as submenus, mirroring the Epic 4 approach

essence: After circling on a fix, David asks when the incident create/edit functionality — working a couple of months ago — actually broke, and whether to 'spray and pray' instrumentation plus an audit of that system area, and whether to commit now.

actions.text: Audit and instrument the new-incident/edit-incident area to find the regression point
